description: A maximum of 16 tunnels can be up simultaneously.

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1060, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX release 10.15.1060, what is the caveat for the 6-in-6 feature?
Feature Caveat: 6-in-6 - A maximum of 16 tunnels can be up simultaneously.

description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1060, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1060, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve the nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp.

description: Symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured. Scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log. Workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.
symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured.
scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log.
workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.

description: Symptom: Error when configuring an username for cloud-managed feature packs if the length of said username is greater than 32 characters. Scenario: When configuring the feature pack server information on a CX switch, configure an username with a length greater than 32 characters. Workaround: Create an username to obtain cloud-managed feature packs from ASP with a length less than 32 characters.
symptom: Error when configuring an username for cloud-managed feature packs if the length of said username is greater than 32 characters.

description: Symptom: Switches may encounter service disruptions or become unresponsive, leading to connectivity loss or requiring reconnection to Central. Scenario: Certain operations, such as dry runs or Central-related activities, can trigger failures or cause system hangs in critical services like restd, smfd and yang-resolverd. These issues can render the system unresponsive, preventing dependent services from re-establishing connectivity.

description: Symptom: The VSX daemon may become unresponsive or hang due to a deadlock condition. Scenario: This issue can occur under rare circumstances when a device with an active peer connection loses that connection and another thread simultaneously attempts to access the peer connection. Workaround: Restart the VSX daemon.
symptom: The VSX daemon may become unresponsive or hang due to a deadlock condition.
scenario: This issue can occur under rare circumstances when a device with an active peer connection loses that connection and another thread simultaneously attempts to access the peer connection.
workaround: Restart the VSX daemon.

description: Symptom: The first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped. Scenario: This issue can occur when no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and client MAC is not learnt yet. Workaround: Subsequent DHCP client retry is successful.
symptom: The first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped.
scenario: This issue can occur when no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and client MAC is not learnt yet.
workaround: Subsequent DHCP client retry is successful.

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1060, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX 10.15.1060, under what scenario does DHCP Relay Bug 392369 occur?
DHCP Relay (Bug ID 392369): This issue can occur when no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and the client MAC address has not been learned yet.

description: Symptom: The first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped. Scenario: This issue can occur when no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and client MAC is not learnt yet. Workaround: Subsequent DHCP client retry is successful.
symptom: The first L2VPN DHCP client packet is dropped.
scenario: This issue can occur when no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients is configured and client MAC is not learnt yet.
workaround: Subsequent DHCP client retry is successful.

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.15.1060, for HPE Aruba AOS-CX 10.15.1060, what is the workaround for DHCP Relay Bug 392369?
DHCP Relay (Bug ID 392369): The documented workaround is: Subsequent DHCP client retry is successful..

description: Symptom: Notification clients stop receiving updates, sessions drop, a core dump is recorded, and the REST service restarts. Scenario: This issue may occur when a subscription is added or removed at the same exact time notifications are being delivered. Workaround: Wait for the REST service to complete its automatic restart, then reconnect the notification clients and reapply the desired subscriptions.

description: Symptom: Error when configuring an username for cloud-managed feature packs if the length of said username is greater than 32 characters. Scenario: When configuring the feature pack server information on a CX switch, configure an username with a length greater than 32 characters. Workaround: Create an username to obtain cloud-managed feature packs from ASP with a length less than 32 characters.
symptom: Error when configuring an username for cloud-managed feature packs if the length of said username is greater than 32 characters.
scenario: When configuring the feature pack server information on a CX switch, configure an username with a length greater than 32 characters.
workaround: Create an username to obtain cloud-managed feature packs from ASP with a length less than 32 characters.

Licensing (Bug ID 388424): The symptom is: Error when configuring an username for cloud-managed feature packs if the length of said username is greater than 32 characters.

description: Symptom: STP ports are going into Disabled/Blocking state. Scenario: After an edge device log-off and log-in on a secured port or when a server connected to VSX pair is rebooted. Workaround: Reboot the PVST process.

workaround: Reboot the PVST process.

description: Symptom: A 500 Internal Server Error occurs when attempting to delete an Aggregate Address using the REST API. Scenario: This issue may occur when trying to delete an Aggregate Address configured under the BGP protocol through the REST API. It could result from specific configurations or interactions within the API.

description: Symptom: The VSX daemon may become unresponsive or hang due to a deadlock condition. Scenario: This issue can occur under rare circumstances when a device with an active peer connection loses that connection and another thread simultaneously attempts to access the peer connection. Workaround: Restart the VSX daemon.
symptom: The VSX daemon may become unresponsive or hang due to a deadlock condition.
scenario: This issue can occur under rare circumstances when a device with an active peer connection loses that connection and another thread simultaneously attempts to access the peer connection.
workaround: Restart the VSX daemon.
